name: CQRS
def: Command Query Responsibility Segregation: separate models for writing (commands) and reading (queries).
problem: Optimise reads and writes independently when their requirements and scale diverge sharply.
mechanism: Commands mutate the write model; read models are separate, denormalised projections updated from write-side events, often asynchronously.
benefits: Independent scaling/optimisation of reads vs writes
failures: Applying CQRS to trivial CRUD (needless complexity)
whenNot: When read and write needs are symmetric
myths: "CQRS requires event sourcing" — they pair well but are independent
